from 33 to 682 tasks through the addition of realistic office worker trajectories

our framework allows the combination of simple oracle functions to generate human-coded ground truths for more complex compositional tasks

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: Our initial release targets three key areas: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), Compliance and Security Operations (CISO), and Financial Operations (FinOps).
The goal of ITBench is to evaluate AI agents on a broad range of real-world IT automation tasks that are otherwise performed by SREs, FinOps, CISO personas.
scenario_domain represents different personas namely "SRE", "CISO", "FinOps" within IT automation.
num_tasks_statement_from_the_paper: ITBench includes an initial set of 94 real-world scenarios, which can be easily extended by community contributions.
Currently, ITBench comprises of an initial set of 94 scenarios spanning across SRE (42), CISO (50), and FinOps (2), with respective successful scenario handling rate of 13.8%, 25.2%, and 0%
Table 2: Exemplar scenario classes and their complexity per scenario domain in ITBench across 94 scenarios.
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: SRE scenarios are based on real-world incidents observed in our own SaaS products
CISO's are on CIS benchmark (for Internet Security, CIS)
scenario_complexity captures the difficulty of a problem and is defined using domain knowledge
SRE scenarios are developed based on real-world incidents observed in our own SaaS products. CISO scenarios are based on CIS benchmark
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: scenario_groundtruth records task-specific outcomes that the Evaluator uses to compare against the agent's expected output
the ground truth for the Diagnosis task includes a list of entities involved in the fault propagation chain, the actual fault propagation chain(s), and fault conditions
the resolution plan outlining a sequence of actions to restore the system to its expected
Resolution plan included trial and error to finally arrive at a state which allowed SRE personnel to execute
ITBench records the final system state, which is then used at the end of all scenario runs along with the pre-defined ground truth data to validate how well the agent performed

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: WorkBench contains a sandbox environment with five databases
We create a sandbox environment consisting of five simulated databases: Calendar, Email, Website analytics, Customer relationship management (CRM), Project management
num_tasks_statement_from_the_paper: WorkBench contains a sandbox environment with five databases, 26 tools, and 690 tasks
We build a dataset of 690 unique tasks by combining human-curated task templates with a programmatic approach for creating multiple tasks per template
Across all domains, we create 10 unique tasks for each template, yielding 690 tasks in total
122 of the 690 tasks in WorkBench do not require any actions to complete
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: We manually create task templates that represent realistic workplace tasks
We build a dataset of 690 unique tasks by combining human-curated task templates with a programmatic approach for creating multiple tasks per template
has_refusal_ability_statement_from_the_p: 122 of the 690 tasks in WorkBench do not require any actions to complete

As shown in Figure 4, 122 of the 690 tasks in WorkBench do not require any actions to complete. The following is an example of such a task: If I haven't met with akira in the last 7 days, schedule a 30 minute meeting with them for the first available slot tomorrow and call it 'catch up'. Otherwise, do nothing.
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: The correct outcome for each task is unique and unambiguous, which allows for robust, automated evaluation. We call this key contribution outcome-centric evaluation
An agent can follow any action path provided the resulting sandbox databases match the ground truth outcome
As shown in Figure 6a, side effects occur when the agent's actions modify the sandbox environment, but this change does not match the ground truth outcome
